title: englerina gabonensis subsp. subquadrangularis
intro: Englerina gabonensis subsp. subquadrangularis is a subspecies of plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Englerina gabonensis subsp. subquadrangularis has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, and has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safe usage.
